methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $46M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Inversion Space
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Material Evolution
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Cobod, a Series A-stage construction technology company, is evaluated within the Housing & Construction sector. The comparable universe consists of three private peers—ICON, Inversion Space, and Material Evolution—with no public company comparables identified. The scarcity of same-stage and same-tier peers in this sector limits direct benchmarking.

Only one valuation method proved applicable to this analysis. The Stage-Progression approach uses Cobod's $46M in total raised capital as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage progression. This yields an implied valuation of $92M. A peer-median approach was not feasible; the Housing & Construction sector yielded only one comparable company, falling below the three-datapoint threshold required for statistical reliability. Attempting a cross-sector median would introduce unacceptable distortion given the specialized nature of construction technology ventures.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $78M (low) to $106M (high), with a median of $92M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single applicable methodology rather than true triangulation across independent approaches. Key uncertainties center on the absence of recent financing or transaction data from sector peers and the nascent stage of the Housing & Construction technology market itself. Any valuation assignment should be treated as provisional pending acquisition of additional comparable transactions or material updates to Cobod's operating metrics.
